expected_app_rationale: No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.
validation_purpose: Well-established ADT + prostate RT combination. Confirm that sequential enzalutamide does not generate a spurious alert.
clinical_concern: False-high alert risk

expected_app_rationale: No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.
validation_purpose: Aromatase inhibitor planned after breast RT. Confirm no false alert for this standard adjuvant sequence.
clinical_concern: False-high alert risk

expected_app_rationale: No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.
validation_purpose: Abiraterone sequential before/after pelvic RT. Confirm appropriate low concern.
clinical_concern: False-high alert risk

expected_app_rationale: No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.
validation_purpose: Bicalutamide with palliative bone RT. Standard low-concern combination; confirm no false alert.
clinical_concern: False-high alert risk

expected_app_rationale: No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.
validation_purpose: Anastrozole planned after chest wall RT. Confirm appropriate low concern for this sequence.
clinical_concern: False-high alert risk

expected_app_rationale: This is a recognised concurrent chemoradiation combination; toxicity risk is expected and should be managed according to the relevant protocol.
validation_purpose: Taxane-based H&N chemoradiation. Confirm Caution (not over-escalated to Moderate/High) for protocol combination. Assess whether mucositis, GI and marrow domains are appropriate.
clinical_concern: Alert level calibration; domain completeness

expected_app_rationale: SRS with immune checkpoint therapy may increase inflammatory imaging changes, oedema or radionecrosis in selected settings.
validation_purpose: Anti-CTLA-4 ICI with intracranial SRS. Assess whether Caution is appropriate, whether CNS oedema/radionecrosis is the correct domain, and whether uncertainty is adequately signalled.
clinical_concern: Alert level; uncertainty signalling; potential for over-reassurance

expected_app_rationale: This is a recognised concurrent chemoradiation combination; toxicity risk is expected and should be managed according to the relevant protocol.
validation_purpose: Docetaxel H&N chemoradiation (curative). Confirm protocol-based Caution. Note: docetaxel lacks CLS-009 classification so IR010 does not fire; only Caution expected, not Moderate.
clinical_concern: Alert calibration; class coverage completeness for taxanes

expected_app_rationale: Concurrent systemic therapy may intensify mucosal toxicity in the irradiated field. This is also a recognised concurrent chemoradiation combination with expected toxicity managed per protocol.
validation_purpose: Cisplatin concurrent H&N chemoradiation — highest-evidence combination in the app. Evaluate whether Moderate (not High) is appropriate for a well-documented radiosensitiser with severe mucositis risk. Assess domain completeness.
clinical_concern: Under-alert risk; domain completeness; nausea/nephrotoxicity not captured in current domains

expected_app_rationale: Concurrent CDK4/6 inhibition may increase marrow and gastrointestinal toxicity, particularly with pelvic or abdominal RT.
validation_purpose: CDK4/6 inhibitor with pelvic RT. Assess whether Moderate is appropriate, and whether GI and marrow domains adequately reflect the risk. Evaluate evidence strength for this class effect.
clinical_concern: Alert level; evidence quality; applicability of class-level rule to individual CDK4/6 agents

expected_app_rationale: Concurrent thoracic RT with targeted kinase inhibition may increase pulmonary toxicity.
validation_purpose: 3rd-generation EGFR TKI with lung SBRT. Assess whether pneumonitis, skin and mucositis domains are appropriate. Evaluate whether Moderate is appropriately calibrated vs High for this combination.
clinical_concern: Alert level; pneumonitis domain; ILD interaction evidence currency

expected_app_rationale: Potential additive interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis risk with thoracic RT.
validation_purpose: T-DXd (trastuzumab deruxtecan) with central lung RT. T-DXd has a specific ILD black-box warning. Assess whether Moderate is appropriately calibrated and whether pneumonitis domain alone is sufficient.
clinical_concern: Alert level; ILD-specific evidence for T-DXd; potential under-alert

expected_app_rationale: The selected systemic therapy and thoracic RT have overlapping pneumonitis/ILD risk.
validation_purpose: Anti-PD-1 ICI with conventional thoracic RT. Assess whether Moderate is appropriate. Note: alert level does not change between conventional and SBRT for this combination (compare with S040).
clinical_concern: Alert level appropriateness; fractionation insensitivity; pneumonitis domain completeness

expected_app_rationale: BRAF inhibition may enhance radiation dermatitis and, rarely, visceral toxicity or radiation recall.
validation_purpose: BRAF inhibitor with skin RT. Assess whether Moderate is appropriate for radiation dermatitis and recall risk. Evaluate evidence basis (single reference EVID-0003) as sufficient for Moderate alert.
clinical_concern: Evidence strength; domain completeness; recall risk signalling

expected_app_rationale: Concurrent systemic therapy may intensify mucosal toxicity in the irradiated field.
validation_purpose: Cetuximab with oesophageal RT at conventional fractionation. Mucositis and GI are triggered via strong mucosal radiosensitiser class. Assess whether this accurately reflects cetuximab-specific toxicity.
clinical_concern: Class vs agent-specific risk; domain completeness; skin toxicity not listed (cetuximab causes acneiform rash)

expected_app_rationale: Concurrent CDK4/6 inhibition may increase marrow and gastrointestinal toxicity, particularly with pelvic or abdominal RT.
validation_purpose: Ribociclib with hypofractionated gynaecological RT. Assess domain completeness and whether evidence for pelvic interaction is adequate.
clinical_concern: Alert level; class extrapolation strength; clinical use context (ribociclib is HR+/HER2- breast cancer agent)

expected_app_rationale: Potential additive marrow suppression from systemic therapy and marrow-exposing RT.
validation_purpose: Gemcitabine with marrow-exposing thoracic spine RT. Only marrow suppression (DOM-06) is returned — not pulmonary toxicity (DOM-05). Assess whether this adequately captures the full risk.
clinical_concern: Domain completeness; gemcitabine has high pulmonary radiosensitisation risk not captured by marrow rule alone

expected_app_rationale: Anti-VEGF pathway inhibition may increase bleeding, perforation, fistula, vascular injury and impaired healing with high-dose RT near hollow viscera or major vessels.
validation_purpose: Bevacizumab with pancreatic SBRT — highest-risk anti-VEGF scenario. Assess whether High is appropriate, domain completeness, and whether fistula/perforation/wound-healing domains are correctly prioritised.
clinical_concern: Alert level; domain completeness; evidence strength for hollow-viscera interaction

expected_app_rationale: Anti-VEGF pathway inhibition may increase bleeding, perforation, fistula, vascular injury and impaired healing with high-dose RT near hollow viscera or major vessels.
validation_purpose: VEGFR TKI with liver SBRT. Assess whether High is appropriate for this combination and whether hepatotoxicity (DOM-13) is a missing domain.
clinical_concern: Alert level; hepatic toxicity domain missing; evidence basis for VEGFR TKI class

expected_app_rationale: Anti-VEGF pathway inhibition may increase bleeding, perforation, fistula, vascular injury and impaired healing with high-dose RT near hollow viscera or major vessels.
validation_purpose: Multi-kinase inhibitor with adrenal SBRT. Assess whether High is appropriate for adrenal SBRT and anti-VEGF combination.
clinical_concern: Alert level for adrenal site; adrenal gland-specific risks not captured

expected_app_rationale: Avoid elective RT during active cytokine-release syndrome, ICANS or unstable immune toxicity.
validation_purpose: CAR-T therapy with whole brain RT. Assess whether High is appropriate for this combination (CRS, ICANS risk context), and whether CNS oedema and marrow suppression domains are correct.
clinical_concern: Alert level; timing rationale (is WBRT typically delivered during active CAR-T therapy?); domain completeness

expected_app_rationale: Avoid elective RT during active cytokine-release syndrome, ICANS or unstable immune toxicity.
validation_purpose: Novel DLL3-targeted bispecific with H&N RT. Tarlatamab's individual agent_alert_level is Caution, but the class rule (CLS-012) fires High. Assess whether High is clinically appropriate for this novel agent in this setting.
clinical_concern: Class vs agent-specific alert level discrepancy; evolving evidence for tarlatamab specifically; domain appropriateness for H&N RT

expected_app_rationale: Anti-VEGF pathway inhibition may increase bleeding, perforation, fistula, vascular injury and impaired healing with high-dose RT near hollow viscera or major vessels.
validation_purpose: Anti-VEGFR2 antibody with GOJ SBRT. Gastric/oesophageal fistula and perforation are well-recognised risks. Assess whether High is appropriate and domain set is complete.
clinical_concern: Alert level; site-specific fistula risk; esophageal/gastric anatomy proximity to SBRT

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: CRITICAL VALIDATION SCENARIO. Gemcitabine has agent_alert_level of High toxicity alert and is classified as a strong pulmonary radiosensitiser, yet the rule engine falls back to Uncertain for lung RT because no class-level lung rule matches gemcitabine's parent classes. Assess whether this is a false reassurance risk and whether a High alert rule is needed.
clinical_concern: Potential false reassurance; gemcitabine + thoracic RT is a high-risk, well-documented combination (radiation pneumonitis risk); this is a candidate rule gap

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: CRITICAL VALIDATION SCENARIO. TMZ concurrent with brain RT is the standard of care for glioblastoma (Stupp protocol) and has well-characterised toxicity. The app produces Uncertain via fallback because TMZ has no parent class assignment. Assess whether Uncertain is acceptable or whether a Caution/Moderate rule is needed.
clinical_concern: Potential false reassurance; standard-of-care protocol not recognized; candidate rule gap

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: PARP inhibitor with pelvic gynaecological RT. PARP inhibitors are DNA damage repair inhibitors used in ovarian cancer; concurrent pelvic RT is an emerging setting. Assess whether Uncertain is appropriate or whether a Moderate alert is warranted.
clinical_concern: Evolving evidence; potential radiosensitisation via DNA repair inhibition; no pelvic-specific PARP rule exists

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: BCL-2 inhibitor with rectal chemoradiation context. Venetoclax has no parent class so falls back to Uncertain. Assess whether this is appropriate for a novel agent with limited RT interaction data.
clinical_concern: Appropriate Uncertain for genuinely limited evidence; assess whether GI domain from agent is correct

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Novel TROP2 ADC (datopotamab deruxtecan) with lung RT. Evolving evidence; assess whether Uncertain is appropriate and whether ILD/pneumonitis (DOM-05) is correctly flagged from agent data.
clinical_concern: Appropriate Uncertain for novel agent; ILD domain from agent data; evolving evidence flag

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Novel claudin 18.2 antibody (used in gastric/GOJ cancer) with oesophageal RT. No class assignment; only DOM-20 (Other site-specific) from agent. Assess whether Uncertain is appropriate and whether the domain placeholder is acceptable.
clinical_concern: Appropriate Uncertain for genuinely novel agent; domain placeholder (DOM-20) appropriateness

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: KRAS G12C inhibitor (sotorasib) with lung SBRT. Novel agent with emerging RT interaction data. Assess whether Uncertain is appropriate and whether pneumonitis (DOM-05) and hepatotoxicity (DOM-13) domains from agent data are clinically relevant.
clinical_concern: Evolving evidence; agent has known ILD risk; appropriate Uncertain vs should this be Moderate

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Bone-modifying agent (bisphosphonate) with spinal RT. Assess whether Uncertain is appropriate, and whether renal toxicity (DOM-12) and osteonecrosis (DOM-16) domains are the correct domains for this agent/site combination.
clinical_concern: Appropriate Uncertain for limited evidence; osteonecrosis (MRONJ) domain relevance to spinal RT context; zoledronic acid comparison

expected_app_rationale: No therapy selected — app returns incomplete state.
validation_purpose: No therapy selected with site and timing. Confirm the app returns an incomplete/prompt state rather than a default alert. Assess whether the incomplete state is clearly communicated.
clinical_concern: Safe handling of missing inputs; no default alert generated

expected_app_rationale: No RT site selected — app returns incomplete state.
validation_purpose: Agent selected (gemcitabine) but no RT site chosen. Confirm incomplete state is returned. Assess whether a partial result or prompt is displayed.
clinical_concern: Safe handling of missing site input; no premature alert generated

expected_app_rationale: Fractionation not specified — app prompts for this field before completing evaluation.
validation_purpose: Nivolumab + lung RT with timing selected but fractionation missing. Assess whether the completeness check fires correctly and whether the fractionation prompt is appropriately displayed.
clinical_concern: Completeness gate; fractionation is required for some rules (IR004); failure to prompt could cause missed alerts

therapy_groups: Immune checkpoint inhibitor — dual ICI (anti-CTLA-4 + anti-PD-1)
expected_app_rationale: The selected systemic therapy and thoracic RT have overlapping pneumonitis/ILD risk. Note: combination-specific interaction effects between ipilimumab and nivolumab are not individually modelled.
validation_purpose: Dual ICI (nivolumab+ipilimumab) with lung SBRT. Each agent independently triggers Moderate via IR011. Assess whether Moderate is appropriate for dual ICI and whether the multi-agent warning is sufficient. Combination effects are not individually modelled.
clinical_concern: Combination effect under-modelling; dual ICI increases pneumonitis risk beyond single-agent ICI; multi-agent warning adequacy

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended. Combination-specific effects not individually modelled.
validation_purpose: CRITICAL VALIDATION SCENARIO. Carboplatin+paclitaxel is a standard concurrent chemoradiation doublet for non-small cell lung cancer. Neither agent has a class rule matching lung central (RTS007). Both fall back to Uncertain. Assess whether this represents a significant clinical gap.
clinical_concern: Potential false reassurance for a standard chemoradiation regimen; rule gap for platinum+taxane in thoracic RT; multi-agent interaction not modelled

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Bevacizumab planned after liver SBRT (TM005). Compare with S018 (concurrent, High). IR003 requires TM001-003 so TM005 does not trigger the high alert. Assess whether the timing-based downgrade from High to Uncertain is clinically appropriate, or whether bevacizumab after SBRT still carries a wound-healing risk.
clinical_concern: Timing-based alert collapse; bevacizumab wound-healing risk may persist after RT; incomplete timing-based rule coverage

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Capecitabine stopped 14 days before starting rectal RT. IR010 and IR009 only fire for TM001 (concurrent). Assess whether Uncertain is appropriate for recent capecitabine with residual radiosensitisation risk, or whether a Caution/Moderate rule should cover TM002.
clinical_concern: Timing gap: recent capecitabine carries residual radiosensitisation risk; TM002 not covered by mucosal radiosensitiser rules; potential under-alert

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: Anthracycline started 21 days after chest wall RT. Doxorubicin is a well-known radiation recall agent; cardiac toxicity overlap with chest wall RT is also recognised. Fallback gives DOM-19 (recall). Assess whether Uncertain is adequate, and whether cardiac toxicity (DOM-11) and radiation recall (DOM-19) domains are appropriately emphasised.
clinical_concern: Radiation recall risk; cardiac toxicity; Uncertain via fallback despite well-characterised recall potential; TM003 not covered by class rules for breast site

expected_app_rationale: The selected systemic therapy and thoracic RT have overlapping pneumonitis/ILD risk.
validation_purpose: Nivolumab with lung SBRT. Compare with S013 (conventional fractionation, same alert). The alert does not escalate with SBRT fractionation for ICI + lung, which may not reflect the potentially higher pneumonitis risk with SBRT. Assess whether fractionation insensitivity is clinically appropriate.
clinical_concern: Fractionation insensitivity for ICI + lung: SBRT may carry higher pneumonitis risk than conventional RT; no SBRT-specific escalation rule for ICIs

expected_app_rationale: Potential additive gastrointestinal toxicity from systemic therapy and bowel irradiation.
validation_purpose: Palbociclib with pelvic SBRT. Compare with S010 (conventional, same agent): conventional gives DOM-01+DOM-06; SBRT gives only DOM-01. The marrow domain (DOM-06) is lost with SBRT because IR002 (which provides DOM-06) requires FX001-003. Assess whether this fractionation-dependent domain loss is clinically appropriate.
clinical_concern: Fractionation-dependent domain change: marrow suppression risk (DOM-06) lost when fractionation is SBRT vs conventional; clinical impact assessment needed

expected_app_rationale: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
validation_purpose: CRITICAL VALIDATION SCENARIO. Trastuzumab is classified under CLS-003 (Anti-VEGF/VEGFR agent) in the data, which does not accurately reflect its mechanism (HER2-directed). No breast-specific rule fires for anti-VEGF class. The fallback returns DOM-11 (cardiac) from agent data, which is clinically relevant but arrived at via fallback. Assess: (1) whether the CLS-003 classification of trastuzumab is appropriate; (2) whether Uncertain is clinically appropriate for concurrent trastuzumab + breast/nodal RT; (3) whether a specific trastuzumab rule is needed.
clinical_concern: Potential misclassification of trastuzumab as anti-VEGF class; cardiac toxicity (DOM-11) domain arrives via fallback only; Uncertain output for a clinically important combination
